source.title: Ultraslow serotonin oscillations in the hippocampus delineate substates across NREM and waking

confidence_rationale: The most directly relevant paper found. Demonstrates in vivo that ultraslow 5-HT oscillations (<0.05 Hz) in the hippocampus create a phase relationship with sharp wave ripples: ripples occur preferentially on the falling phase of 5-HT oscillations during NREM, with higher-power ripples clustering near the peak during NREM specifically. This is precisely the mechanism MECH-203 requires -- 5-HT level at the time of consolidation determines replay salience, with falling 5-HT phases permitting and prioritizing higher-power (more content-rich) SWRs. Source quality high (eLife peer-reviewed, in vivo). Mapping fidelity high: the ripple-phase relationship is exactly the gating mechanism MECH-203 predicts.
failure_signatures: Ripple phase-locking to falling 5-HT is demonstrated during NREM and wake -- not specific to NREM only, which weakens the sleep-specific replay consolidation claim

Higher-power ripples cluster near the 5-HT peak (not falling phase) during NREM -- this means the highest-salience replay may occur when 5-HT is still elevated, not at the trough; the direction is partially counterintuitive
Arousal markers (microarousals, EMG peaks) also cluster on the rising phase -- 5-HT oscillations may be tracking arousal substates rather than specifically regulating memory consolidation
The specific episodes that are replayed during falling-5-HT phases (whether benefit-dense vs harm-dense) are not characterised

mapping.source_claim_statement: Hippocampal 5-HT shows ultraslow (<0.05 Hz) oscillations during NREM and waking. Hippocampal ripples occur preferentially on the falling phase of these oscillations in both NREM and wake. Higher-power ripples (stronger replay events) concentrate near the 5-HT peak during NREM. Arousal markers concentrate on the rising phase. Hippocampal-cortical coherence is strongest on the rising phase. The oscillations temporally segregate consolidation (falling phase) from arousal-related processing (rising phase).
mapping.ree_translation: MECH-203 proposes that tonic 5-HT level gates replay eligibility, with high 5-HT suppressing and low 5-HT permitting replay. This paper demonstrates in vivo that hippocampal ripples cluster on the falling phase of 5-HT oscillations during NREM -- the falling-5-HT window is the replay window. The finding that higher-power ripples cluster near the 5-HT peak during NREM is more complex: it may indicate that the onset of falling 5-HT (from a high waking baseline) primes the highest-salience replay first, consistent with benefit-dense experiences (occurring at high waking 5-HT) being the first to replay when 5-HT begins falling. The REE SerotoninModule's transition from waking to sleep_mode produces exactly this falling-phase dynamic. The segregation of arousal (rising 5-HT) from consolidation (falling 5-HT) also maps to the REE distinction between active exploration (high 5-HT, benefit_salience update) and offline consolidation (falling 5-HT, replay execution).
mapping.mapping_caveat: The ripple-phase relationship is found during both NREM and waking, not sleep-specific. The specific replay content (which memories are replayed) is not characterised -- only ripple power, not episode identity. The higher-power ripples near the 5-HT peak is counterintuitive relative to simple masking model (Haq 2016) and may indicate that NREM 5-HT oscillations implement a more complex replay scheduling logic than MECH-203 currently specifies.
mapping.source_context: In vivo recording in mice. Implanted electrodes in hippocampus with serotonin biosensors. Combined ripple detection and ultraslow 5-HT oscillation measurement during natural sleep and waking. The study is recent (2025) and represents the most direct evidence of hippocampal 5-HT oscillation -- ripple phase coupling available.

confidence_components.notes: Source quality very high: eLife, in vivo, direct measurement of hippocampal 5-HT with simultaneous ripple detection. Mapping fidelity high: the ripple-5-HT phase relationship is exactly the MECH-203 mechanism. Transfer risk low-moderate: mouse, but hippocampal replay and serotonergic modulation are conserved. Minor uncertainty about direction of higher-power ripple clustering.
